zh.lifeSummary: 當John de Saye II於1381年出生在英國賀特福爾布洛布恩時，他的父親John Say38歲，母親Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem36歲。他與Maud Broxbourne結婚。他們至少是4個兒子的父母。他去世於1478年4月12日在他的家鄉享年97歲，埋葬在英國賀特福爾布洛布恩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John de Saye II于1381年出生在英国賀特福爾布洛布恩时，他的父亲John Say38岁，母亲Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem36岁。他与Maud Broxbourne结婚。他们至少是4个儿子的父母。他去世于1478年4月12日在他的家乡享年97岁，埋葬在英国賀特福爾布洛布恩。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Saye
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized form of Slovenian Saje: nickname for someone with very dark, sooty hair, derived from the root of sajast ‘sooty’, or directly from saja ‘soot’.
West African (Liberia): from the personal name Saye, found among the Mano (Mah) people, denoting a first-born boy.

variants.name: Say
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Sai in Orne or Say in Indre, France, perhaps so called from a Gaulish personal name Saius + the Latin locative suffix -acum (see also French name 3 below).

English: nickname, perhaps for someone who made or sold say, a type of woollen cloth, or for an habitual wearer of clothes made of this material, from Middle English sai(e) ‘say’ (Old French saie, from Latin saga, plural of sagum ‘military cloak’). See also Sayer .

French (southern): topographic name from saix (from Latin saxum) ‘rock’, or a habitational name from a place called with this word, for example, Say in Loire, Saix in Tarn and Vienne, Le Saix in Hautes-Alpes, or Les Saix in Isère.

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When John de Saye II was born in 1381, in Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England, his father, John Say, was 38 and his mother, Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, was 36. He married Maud Broxbourne. They were the parents of at least 4 sons. He died on 12 April 1478, in his hometown, at the age of 97, and was buried in Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John de Saye II est né en 1381, à Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Angleterre, son père, John Say, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, avait 36 ans. Il a épousé Maud Broxbourne. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils. Il est décédé le 12 avril 1478, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 97 ans, et a été enterré à Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Angleterre.

de.lifeSummary: Als John de Saye II 1381 in Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Say 38 und seine Mutter Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem 36. Er hat Maud Broxbourne geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 4 Söhne. Er starb am 12. April 1478 im Alter von 97 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John de Saye II è nato il nel 1381 nel Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England, il suo padre, John Say, aveva 38 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, aveva 36 anni. Lui sposato Maud Broxbourne. Erano genitori di almeno 4 figli. Morì il 12 aprile 1478, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 97 anni, e fu sepolto a Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, England.

ja.lifeSummary: John de Saye IIが1381年にイギリスハートフォードシュアイアブロクスボウルンで生まれた時，父John Sayは38歳，母Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wemは36歳であった。Maud Broxbourneと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも4人の息子の親であった。彼は1478年4月12日に故郷で97歳で死去し，イギリスハートフォードシュアイアブロクスボウルンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John de Saye II(이)가 1381년 영국 허트퍼드실르 브록스보우른에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Say(은)는 38세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem(은)는 36세였다. 그는 Maud Broxbourne(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1478년 4월 12일 그의 고향에서 97세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 허트퍼드실르 브록스보우른에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John de Saye II nasceu em 1381, em Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, John Say, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, tinha 36 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Broxbourne. Eles tiveram pelo menos 4 filhos. Ele faleceu em 12 de abril de 1478, em sua cidade natal, com 97 anos, e foi sepultado em Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John de Saye II родился в 1381 г. в местности броксбурн, хиртфордшир, Англия, его отцу по имени John Say было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, 36. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Broxbourne. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына. Он умер 12 апреля 1478 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 97 лет и был похоронен в местности броксбурн, хиртфордшир, Англия.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John de Saye II nació en 1381, en Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra, su papá, John Say, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Elizabeth le Boteler 4th Baroness Boteler of Wem, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Maud Broxbourne. Fueron padres de por lo menos 4 hijos. Murió el 12 de abril de 1478, en su pueblo, a la edad de 97 años, y fue sepultado en Broxbourne, Hertfordshire, Inglaterra.
